cards.back: Refers to the existence of pre-existing prejudices or discriminatory patterns in AI algorithms, which can perpetuate existing health disparities and inequalities
The process by which patients are fully aware of the risks and benefits of participating in AI-driven healthcare research or treatment, and provide voluntary consent
The measures taken to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive patient data used in AI-driven healthcare applications
The development of AI models that provide transparent and interpretable explanations for their decisions, enabling trust in AI-driven healthcare outcomes

cards.front: Regulatory Framework for AI in Healthcare
cards.back: A set of laws, guidelines, and standards that govern the development, deployment, and use of AI in healthcare, ensuring safety, efficacy, and accountability
